arguments.task: Research Ecwid using official sources. Visit https://api-docs.ecwid.com first, then find and capture official Ecwid developer documentation pages for API overview, authentication, and rate limits. Also find official Ecwid pricing or plan pages indicating API availability/access requirements. Then find evidence of any official MCP server or first-party GitHub/MCP registry listing for Ecwid; if none is found on official sources, search reputable MCP registry/GitHub pages for Ecwid MCP. Return concise extracted findings with exact source URLs for each claim.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Ecwid docs and access pages, plus MCP evidence.

arguments.task: Open and extract exact evidence from these likely official Ecwid pages: https://docs.ecwid.com/api-reference , https://docs.ecwid.com/get-started/set-up-your-dev-environment-in-ecwid , and if available the OAuth page under docs.ecwid.com. Capture exact statements about REST API, GraphQL support if any, auth methods for private/custom apps and public apps, and rate limits. Also open https://www.ecwid.com/pricing and capture plan/API access details. Finally determine whether an official Ecwid MCP server exists by searching ecwid.com/docs.ecwid.com and Ecwid GitHub/official channels; if not found, note absence and search GitHub/MCP registry for third-party Ecwid MCP evidence. Return a concise final report with exact URLs.

input.thought: Launch a narrower extraction task to get exact source-backed findings for Ecwid docs, pricing, and MCP.

raw_result.description: Ecommerce platform API for managing online stores, products, orders, customers, and app integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Ecwid docs state public apps use OAuth 2.0, while custom/private apps can use pre-generated access tokens and skip the OAuth flow.
raw_result.gating_reason: Ecwid docs say only stores on paid plans can access the API, though developers can request a free upgrade for development stores.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documentation covers core ecommerce resources and app integrations; evidence gathered did not verify GraphQL support.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Ecwid MCP server was verified from the gathered official-source evidence; third-party MCP status was not confirmed.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears limited to paid plans for production stores, with a developer-upgrade workaround for development environments.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates a rate limit of 600 requests per minute per token, but the exact canonical rate-limit page/section was not separately verified in this run.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer docs are public and substantial; Ecwid distinguishes private/custom vs public apps, and the docs explicitly mention OAuth 2.0 plus token-based access for custom apps.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Pricing/access page and MCP absence were not directly verified from final extracted pages in this run, and GraphQL non-support was inferred only from lack of evidence rather than an explicit official statement.
